overview: Group related asset variants (e.g. hero/banner/mobile sizes of the same image) so the browse grid shows one representative card per content family instead of flooding the grid with near-identical thumbnails. Clicking a grouped card opens the detail modal with a variant switcher strip.
goals: Browse grid defaults to showing one primary asset per variant group, with a '+N variants' badge
AssetDetailModal shows a horizontal variant thumbnail strip when the asset belongs to a group
Clicking a variant thumbnail in the strip switches the modal to show that variant's details
Four known variant groups are seeded in the existing dataset
nonGoals: No automatic variant detection for future uploads — grouping is set via the variant_group_id field
No UI to create or edit variant groups
No changes to the upload flow
successMetrics: Browse grid shows ~36 cards instead of 50 when in grouped mode (4 groups collapsed)
Each grouped card shows a '+N variants' badge with the correct count
Opening a grouped asset shows a thumbnail strip with all variants
Clicking a variant thumbnail updates the modal image, title, metadata, and CDN URL
The 'Show All Variants' toggle on the card expands to flat view

routes.name: Get Asset Variants
routes.purpose: Returns all assets that share the same variant_group_id as the given asset. Returns empty array if asset has no variant_group_id.

uiNotes: AssetCard: when asset.variant_count > 1, show a small pill badge bottom-right of the thumbnail: '+N variants', navy background (#001B6B), white text, font-size 11px, padding 2px 8px, border-radius 10px

AssetDetailModal left panel: when variants array has length > 1, render a horizontal thumbnail strip below the main image. Strip is scrollable (overflow-x:auto), shows up to 5 thumbs at once. Each thumb: 72px square, object-fit:cover, border-radius 4px, cursor:pointer. Active thumb has 2px navy border. Below each thumb: a channel/format label in 10px grey text (e.g. 'hero', 'banner', 'landscape').
Switching variant: clicking a thumb re-fetches GET /api/assets/:id for that variant, updates all modal state (image, title, metadata rows, quality issues, CDN URL). The selected asset id changes.
Variant strip header: small label 'Variants (N)' in grey 12px text above the strip
No 'Show All Variants' toggle needed on the card — grouped mode is the default view

dataModel.description: Add two columns for variant grouping
fields: variant_group_id TEXT — shared ID for all variants in a group, NULL if no variants
is_primary_variant INTEGER DEFAULT 0 — 1 for the card shown in the browse grid

rules: Migration: ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN variant_group_id TEXT; ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN is_primary_variant INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
Seed the four known groups after migration:
Group grp-star-icon: all assets with subcategory='star-and-icon'. Primary: 1040x520-2x.jpg
Group grp-bankroll: filenames starting with 'Bankroll' or 'BankrollBlitz'. Primary: BankrollBlitz_Desktop .jpg
Group grp-naples: filenames containing 'naples-italy'. Primary: allure-of-the-seas-naples-italy.jpg (landscape)
GET /api/assets default behaviour: WHERE (is_primary_variant = 1 OR variant_group_id IS NULL) — returns one card per group plus all ungrouped assets
Each asset in the default response includes variant_count: count of assets sharing the same variant_group_id (1 if no group)
GET /api/assets?show_all_variants=1 returns all assets flat (existing behaviour)

GET /api/assets/:id/variants returns all assets WHERE variant_group_id = (SELECT variant_group_id FROM assets WHERE id = ?) AND variant_group_id IS NOT NULL, ordered by is_primary_variant DESC then filename ASC
variant_count is computed in the search service via a subquery or join, not stored
FTS5 index does not need to be updated — variant_group_id is not a searchable field

stories.title: DB migration — add variant_group_id and seed known groups
stories.description: As a developer, I want variant group IDs stored in the database so the API can filter and group assets reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/db/schema.js adds variant_group_id TEXT and is_primary_variant INTEGER DEFAULT 0 to the CREATE TABLE statement
A migration script server/src/db/migrate-variants.js runs ALTER TABLE if columns don't exist, then seeds the four groups
server/src/db/index.js runs the migration on startup (require and call it after db init)

After migration: 4 assets have is_primary_variant=1, all galveston assets share grp-galveston, all star-and-icon assets share grp-star-icon, all bankroll assets share grp-bankroll, both naples assets share grp-naples
Supertest test: GET /api/assets returns fewer than 50 assets by default (grouped mode)
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?show_all_variants=1 returns all 50 assets

stories.title: API — grouped browse and variants endpoint
stories.description: As a developer, I want the search API to default to grouped mode and expose a variants endpoint so the UI can show grouped cards and a variant switcher.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets default: WHERE (is_primary_variant = 1 OR variant_group_id IS NULL)
Each asset in response includes variant_count (integer, minimum 1)
GET /api/assets?show_all_variants=1 returns all assets with no grouping filter
GET /api/assets/:id/variants returns array of all assets in the same group, empty array if no group
client/src/api/assets.js exports getAssetVariants(id) that calls GET /api/assets/:id/variants
Supertest test: GET /api/assets returns asset with variant_count=4 for the galveston primary
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/:id/variants for a galveston asset returns 4 items
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/:id/variants for an ungrouped asset returns []

stories.title: AssetCard — variant count badge
stories.description: As a content author, I want to see how many variants an asset has so I know there are more options available.
acceptanceCriteria: AssetCard renders a '+N variants' badge when asset.variant_count > 1
Badge positioned bottom-right of the thumbnail, overlaid with position:absolute
Badge style: navy background (#001B6B), white text, font-size 11px, padding 2px 8px, border-radius 10px
Badge text: '+N variants' where N = variant_count - 1 (e.g. 4 total → '+3 variants')
No badge shown when variant_count === 1 or variant_count is undefined
Vitest test: card with variant_count=4 renders badge with text '+3 variants'
Vitest test: card with variant_count=1 does not render a badge

stories.title: AssetDetailModal — variant thumbnail strip
stories.description: As a content author, I want to see and switch between all variants of an asset in the detail modal so I can find the right format without closing the modal.
acceptanceCriteria: On open, if asset has variant_group_id set, call getAssetVariants(id) and store variants in local state
If variants.length > 1, render a variant strip in the left panel below the main image
Strip header: 'Variants (N)' label, 12px grey text
Strip: horizontal scrollable row (overflow-x:auto, display:flex, gap:8px)
Each thumb: 72px × 72px img (object-fit:cover, border-radius:4px, cursor:pointer), with a channel label below (10px, #6b7280)
Active/selected thumb has 2px solid navy border
Clicking a thumb calls getAsset(variantId), updates asset state — all modal content updates (image, title, metadata, CDN URL, quality issues)
Initially selected thumb is the currently opened asset
If getAssetVariants returns [] or errors, strip is not rendered
Vitest test: modal fetches variants on open when asset has variant_group_id
Vitest test: clicking a variant thumb calls getAsset with the variant's id
Vitest test: no strip rendered when variants array is empty
